---
title: "Central America Trip — March 2026"
subtitle: "Guatemala & Belize | March 15–25, 2026"
date: "March 1, 2026"
---

## Day-by-Day Itinerary

### Sunday, March 15 — Travel Day (Grand Rapids → Tikal)

**Flight 1 — United UA 2099**

- Depart: GRR (Grand Rapids) at **7:25 AM**
- Arrive: ORD (Chicago O'Hare) at **7:37 AM**
- Connection: 1h 24m

**Flight 2 — United UA 2006**

- Depart: ORD (Chicago) at **9:01 AM**
- Arrive: GUA (Guatemala City) at **12:41 PM**

::: {.callout-tip}
**Cash:** Pull Q800 from the ATM at Guatemala City airport on arrival — covers the hostel plus meals in Flores.
:::

**Flight 3 — TagAirlines 5U150**

- Check-in: **2:40 PM** at GUA
- Depart: GUA at **4:10 PM**
- Arrive: FRS (Flores) at **5:10 PM**

*Evening: Arrive FRS ~5:10 PM. Take lodge shuttle to Tikal (~1 hr). Check in, dinner at the lodge, sleep to howler monkeys.*

**Jungle Lodge — Reservation #2041175675302**

- Status: ✅ Confirmed — spoke with Sulema via WhatsApp March 8
- Payment: $132.98 total (room $109 + taxes) — charged in full ~March 10 (5 days before arrival). **Free cancellation until March 10 only — no refund after.**
- Check-in: 3:00 PM | Check-out: 11:00 AM
- Shuttle: FRS airport → lodge ($19/person one-way) — awaiting confirmation
- Contact: [info@junglelodgetikal.com](mailto:info@junglelodgetikal.com) | +502 7861 0447

---

### Monday, March 16 — Tikal National Park → Flores

- **4:00–8:00 AM — Sunrise Tour** ($16/person + ~$20 park entry cash at gate) — confirm with hotel
  - ⚠️ **Buy park tickets before entering** — no tickets sold inside the park, sunrise tickets must be purchased in advance
  - Up to 80% chance of cloud cover in the morning — mist is likely but the experience is still worth it
  - Bring bug spray, water, snacks, and sun protection
- **Key sights at Tikal:**
  - **Templo IV** — one of the few temples you can climb; commanding views across the jungle canopy; the filming location for Star Wars: A New Hope
  - **Gran Plaza** — Templo I & II facing each other; clap your hands in the center to hear a quetzal-like echo; climb Templo II for photos
  - **El Mundo Perdido** — astronomical observatory with Teotihuacán-style architecture
  - **Acrópolis del Norte** — funerary complex, richly detailed
  - Birders: ramón trees in the Gran Plaza attract aracari toucans and slaty-tailed trogons
- Full morning in the ruins — the site is vast, easy to find quieter corners away from tour groups
- **Check out by 11:00 AM** — shuttle from lodge to Flores island (~1 hr)
- Check into **Los Amigos Hostel** — Booking ID #45456, pay Q600 cash at check-in
  - Check-in: 1:00 PM | Check-out: 10:00 AM
  - Contact: [amigoshostel@gmail.com](mailto:amigoshostel@gmail.com) · +502 4495 2399
  - King room with lake and jungle views, private bathroom
- **Afternoon options in Flores** (if energy allows after Tikal):
  - **Jorge's Rope Swing** (Q25, 9am–7pm) — swing into the lake, sunbathe, decompress
  - **El Mirador del Rey Canek** — lookout atop an unexcavated Maya structure, accessible by lancha (skiff) from the Flores waterfront
  - Walk the island loop in 30 minutes, browse shops and cafes
- **Eating in Flores:**
  - **Maracuyá** — traditional Guatemalan food with cocktails overlooking the lake, open until 10pm Wed–Mon
  - Secret Garden resto-bar at Los Amigos — best margaritas on the island, open until 1am
- **Drinks:** Mangos Bar — climb three flights for a west-facing rooftop with unparalleled sunset views and excellent cocktails (noon–10pm)

::: {.callout-tip}
**Tonight:** Book your Flores → San Ignacio shuttle through the Los Amigos travel agency — they sell bus tickets in all directions, open until 10 PM. Shuttles depart from Santa Elena and fill up — easiest to arrange here.
:::

---

### Tuesday, March 17 — Flores → San Ignacio, Belize

- Morning: Shuttle from Flores/Santa Elena toward the Belize border (~$25-35, 3-4 hrs including border crossing). Book through Los Amigos travel agency the night before — they sell tickets in all directions and shuttles depart from Santa Elena. Alternatively ask Amber at Bella's.
- Cross at **Benque Viejo / Melchor de Mencos** — have passport ready, expect a wait
- Arrive San Ignacio — check into **Bella's Backpackers**, Booking #44298
  - 4 Galves St, San Ignacio | Private room with private bathroom
  - WhatsApp: +501 632-8868
- **Breakfast options in San Ignacio:**
  - **Pop's Restaurant** — Belizean breakfast institution, fry-jacks, flour tortillas, open 6am–3pm
  - **Martha's Kitchen** — local Belizean breakfast, hash fish, longaniza eggs, 6am–3pm
  - **Running W** (San Ignacio Resort Hotel) — international menu, birding at breakfast — aracaris perch nearby, 7am–9pm
- **San Ignacio Market** (5am–5pm, best on Saturdays) — Maya, Mennonite, Creole and Mestizo vendors; local produce, crafts, cheap street food. Worth a wander even midweek.
- Afternoon: Take it easy or pick one activity:
  - **Cahal Pech ruins** (6am–6pm) — 5-min drive or uphill walk, dates to 1200 BCE, one of oldest Maya settlements in Belize. Self-guided 1.5 hrs. Great birding (motmots, trogons). Sunset views from the A1 temple over town.
  - **Xunantunich ruins** — 30 min away, massive site with El Castillo pyramid and incredible views, hand-cranked river ferry
  - **Tubing on the Macal or Mopan River** — lazy jungle float, great way to meet people

---

### Wednesday, March 18 — ATM Cave (Actun Tunichil Muknal)

**Mayawalk Tours — Order #R0SAMLP** ✅ Confirmed

- Meet at Mayawalk office, **19 Burns Avenue, San Ignacio** at **7:30 AM** (between Tandoor Restaurant and Belize Bank)
- 45-min drive to Tapir Mountain Nature Reserve, 45-min jungle hike to cave entrance
- ~3 hours inside — swimming, wading, climbing through chambers to the Crystal Maiden
- Lunch included (non-vegetarian), back in San Ignacio by ~4:30 PM
- Balance due on day: **$73.77**

::: {.callout-tip}
**What to wear/bring:**

- Hiking shoes/trainers with closed toes (will get wet) + flip flops to change into
- Socks (mandatory)
- Shorts and shirt — **no sleeveless shirts, tank tops, or bikini tops** (dress code enforced)
- Extra dry clothes for after
- Small backpack, reusable water bottle, bug spray, snacks
- Printed waiver (front and back on one sheet) + valid ID
- No cameras or electronics — government ban. Stock photos emailed to you afterward from the Mayawalk office.
:::

---

### Thursday, March 19 — San Ignacio → Caye Caulker

- **Morning** — Shuttle from San Ignacio to Belize City Marine Terminal ⚠️ Not booked
- **~Midday** — Arrive Belize City, water taxi to Caye Caulker (~45 min) — buy ticket on the day
- **Afternoon** — Check in to overnight accommodation on Caye Caulker ⚠️ Not booked
- Evening: Sunset at The Split, dinner on the island

---

### Friday, March 20 — Caye Caulker (Airbnb check-in)

- **3:00–5:00 PM** — Check in at Sunrise Scuba office, Avenida Hicaco (required before first dive)
- **3:00 PM** — Check in at Airbnb hosted by Norm, Playa Asuncion | Confirmation: **HMDYZAD4J2**
- Relax, explore the island

---

### Saturday–Tuesday, March 21–24 — Caye Caulker

*Relax at the Airbnb, dive, and enjoy the island.*

**Diving — Sunrise Scuba** ✅ Deposit sent March 8

[sunrisescubabz@gmail.com](mailto:sunrisescubabz@gmail.com) · [sunrisescubabelize.com](https://www.sunrisescubabelize.com)

| Date | Dive | Deposit | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| Saturday, March 21 | Esmeralda | $25 | ✅ Deposit sent |
| Sunday, March 22 | Local Reef | $25 | ✅ Deposit sent |
| Monday, March 23 | Blue Hole | $50 | ✅ Deposit sent |
| Tuesday, March 24 | *(not booked)* | — | — |

**Other activities:**

- **The Split** — The island's social hub; Lazy Lizard Bar & Grill (pool, bar, diving platform), Sip N Dip overwater bar. Full Moon Parties monthly.
- **Tarpon Feeding Dock** — Free. Calle La Posa, opposite the street BBQs. Dozens of massive tarpons (up to 8ft) swim below the dock. Remarkable and free.
- **Stingray beach** at Iguana Reef Inn — Daily 4pm to sunset, dozens of stingrays arrive in the shallows. Seahorse reserve nearby. Skip the feeding, but worth watching.
- **BBQs near the Split** — Jerk chicken, shrimp kebabs, whole fish, grilled lobster. Betty's, Ester's, Chef Kareem's. Great prices, fire up from lunchtime.
- **Snorkeling:** Hol Chan Marine Reserve & Shark Ray Alley (most popular); Caye Caulker Marine Reserve (coral gardens, the Swash, Shark Ray Village). Operators: Caveman Snorkeling Tours, Nauti Time Tours, Stressless Eco Friendly Tours.
- **Manatee tour at Swallow Caye Wildlife Sanctuary** — 19 miles away, guided boat tour, sightings not guaranteed but often rewarded. Ask any operator.
- **Kayaking through the mangroves** — Calm west side of island. Good Vibes Tours rents clear-bottomed kayaks.
- **Eating:** Amor y Café (breakfast, 6:15am–12:15pm), Errolyn's House of Fry Jacks (local breakfast staple), Maggie's Sunset Kitchen (Belizean seafood, sunset dock, 11:30am–2pm & 5–9pm), Meldy's Restaurant (local Belizean), Il Pellicano Cucina Italiana (garden Italian, pizzas and pastas, 5–9:30pm), Pasta per Caso (Italian, reserve ahead via WhatsApp 602-6670, Wed–Sun dinner)
- **Drinking:** The Pelican Sunset Bar (sunset drinks), I&I Reggae Bar (three levels, hammocks, panoramic views, 4pm–2am Mon–Sat), Taproom (only brewery on the island, beer flights, 10:30am–11pm)

---

### Wednesday, March 25 — Fly Home

- **Check out: 10:00 AM** from Airbnb
- **~11:00–11:30 AM** — Water taxi: Caye Caulker → Belize City (~45 min)
- **~12:15–12:30 PM** — Arrive Belize City, transfer to Philip Goldson International Airport (BZE)

**Flight 1 — American Airlines AA 356**

- Depart: BZE at **1:50 PM**
- Arrive: MIA (Miami) at **6:05 PM**
Connection: 1h 54m

**Flight 2 — American Airlines AA 1678**

- Depart: MIA at **7:59 PM**
- Arrive: GRR (Grand Rapids) at **11:28 PM**

---

## Still To Book

- [ ] **Travel eSIM** — get before departure. Airalo or Holafly; look for a regional Central America plan covering both Guatemala and Belize.
- [ ] **Confirm Gus stay** — March 14–15 (arrange sitter). Caitlin covers remaining days.
- [ ] **Flores → San Ignacio shuttle, March 17** — asked Amber at Bella's (awaiting reply). Alternate: book at Los Amigos front desk March 16 evening
- [ ] **San Ignacio → Belize City Marine Terminal shuttle, March 19** — asked Amber at Bella's (awaiting reply). Timing-sensitive — need enough buffer to catch the water taxi. Alternates if Bella's can't do it:
  - **William's Belize Shuttle** — [williamshuttlebelize.com](https://williamshuttlebelize.com)
  - **San Ignacio Shuttles** (Sergio/Jamie) — [sanignacioshuttles.com](https://www.sanignacioshuttles.com)
- [ ] **March 19 overnight on Caye Caulker** — asked Amber about Bella's Caye Caulker sister hostel (discount available as Bella's guest; LP calls it a backpacker hideaway with free bikes/kayaks, must book ahead). Alternate: **DreamCabanas** (9.2 score, 309 reviews, ~$110/night). LP also recommends Barefoot Beach Belize (beachfront cottages, kitchenettes, free bikes, good value $) and Island Magic Beach Resort (beachfront, pool, restaurant $$).

---

## Bookings at a Glance

| Booking | Confirmation | Details |
|---|---|---|
| United Airlines (GRR → GUA) | **O0P1XY** | March 15 |
| TagAirlines (GUA → FRS) | **00IZ4F** | March 15 |
| Jungle Lodge, Tikal | **2041175675302** | March 15–16 ✅ Confirmed |
| Los Amigos Hostel, Flores | **45456** | March 16–17, pay Q600 cash |
| Bella's Backpackers, San Ignacio | **44298** | March 17–19 (checkout March 19) |
| Caye Caulker — March 19 night | ⚠️ Not booked | Bella's CC (discount via Amber) or DreamCabanas |
| Mayawalk Tours — ATM Cave | **R0SAMLP** | March 18, 8:00 AM |
| Airbnb – Caye Caulker | **HMDYZAD4J2** | March 20–25, hosted by Norm |
| Sunrise Scuba — 3 dives | ✅ Deposit sent | Mar 21 Esmeralda · Mar 22 Local Reef · Mar 23 Blue Hole |
| American Airlines (BZE → GRR) | **DZGOPI** | March 25 |

---

## Key Contacts

| | Confirmation / Contact | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| United Airlines | O0P1XY | GRR→GUA, March 15 |
| TagAirlines | 00IZ4F | GUA→FRS, March 15 |
| Jungle Lodge | 2041175675302 | [info@junglelodgetikal.com](mailto:info@junglelodgetikal.com) · WhatsApp +502 3185 0223 |
| Los Amigos Hostel | 45456 | Flores, pay Q600 cash |
| Bella's Backpackers | 44298 | WhatsApp +501 632-8868 |
| Mayawalk Tours | R0SAMLP | [reservations@mayawalk.com](mailto:reservations@mayawalk.com) · +501 824-3070 |
| Airbnb (Caye Caulker) | HMDYZAD4J2 | Hosted by Norm |
| Sunrise Scuba | Jerrison | [sunrisescubabz@gmail.com](mailto:sunrisescubabz@gmail.com) · deposit sent March 8 |
| American Airlines | DZGOPI | BZE→GRR, March 25 |

---

## Packing List

### Documents
- [ ] Passport
- [ ] Printed ATM Cave waiver (front and back, **single sheet only**)
- [ ] Completed NICH visitor information form
- [ ] Booking confirmations (digital or printed)

### Pack Setup
- [ ] Main bag / travel pack
- [x] Osprey Ultralight Stuff Pack 18L (day trips, Tikal, ATM Cave) ✅ Ordered
- [ ] Dry bag (water taxis, boat dives, ATM Cave)
- [ ] Lightweight packing cubes
- [ ] Plastic bags (laundry separation, wet gear)

### Clothing
- [ ] Quick-dry shirts
- [ ] Merino or quick-dry long-sleeve layer (evenings, AC, jungle bug protection)
- [ ] Shorts — 2–3
- [ ] Swimsuit(s) — 2 (one always drying)
- [ ] Light rain jacket
- [ ] Quick-dry underwear
- [ ] One nicer shirt for dinners out
- [ ] Travel pants
- [ ] Socks

### Footwear
- [ ] Old closed-toe shoes (ATM Cave — will get completely soaked, don't bring nice ones)
- [ ] Flip flops / sandals

### Toiletries
- [ ] Toothbrush + toothpaste
- [ ] Shampoo (travel size)
- [ ] Dr. Bronner's (body wash + laundry soap)
- [ ] Deodorant
- [ ] Razor + shaving cream
- [ ] Quick-dry travel towel
- [ ] Reef-safe sunscreen (required for Caye Caulker marine reserve)
- [ ] Lip balm with SPF
- [ ] Bug spray (essential for Tikal and ATM Cave)
- [ ] Contacts and solution
- [ ] Nail clippers

### Health & First Aid
- [ ] Celexa
- [ ] Ambien
- [ ] Ibuprofen / acetaminophen
- [ ] Imodium (antidiarrheal)
- [ ] Hand sanitizer

### Gear & Accessories
- [ ] Sunglasses (prescription and non prescription)
- [ ] Hat
- [ ] Reusable water bottle
- [ ] Dry bag (water taxis, boat dives, ATM Cave)
- [ ] Scuba mask and snorkel
- [ ] Pen (border crossings, customs forms)
- [ ] Clothesline (drying swimsuits and dive gear)
- [ ] Headlamp + extra batteries (Tikal sunrise hike in darkness)
- [ ] Snacks for travel days and ATM Cave

### Electronics
- [ ] Phone + charger
- [ ] AirPods
- [ ] Kindle + charger
- [ ] MacBook + charger
- [ ] Power bank
- [ ] Dive computer (considering purchase)

### Money
- [ ] USD cash — works throughout Belize
- [ ] Credit/debit cards

---

*Last updated March 8, 2026*
